idea-plugin.name: DevGlobe
idea-plugin.description:
Appear on a 3D globe in real time while you code. Your activity shows up live on devglobe.app — other developers can see you, discover your projects, and find your links.

How it works

  1. Sign in on devglobe.app with GitHub, X (Twitter), or Google
  2. Copy your API key from settings
  3. Open the DevGlobe tool window in your IDE and paste the key
  4. You appear on the globe

The extension sends a heartbeat every 30 seconds while you code. It pauses automatically after 1 minute of inactivity. After 10 minutes without activity, you disappear from the globe.

Features

  • Live heartbeat — your presence on the globe in real time
  • Language detection — uses your IDE's native FileType system, supports all languages
  • Git integration — detects your repo (commit data is never read or sent by the plugin)
  • Status message — tell others what you're working on
  • Repo sharing — you decide whether your repo name is visible (off by default)
  • Status bar — your coding time today displayed live
  • Notifications — native IDE notifications for every action

What DevGlobe gives you

  • Enhanced public profile — GitHub, X, projects, activity, tech stack and links on a single shareable page
  • Project directory — publish projects, invite teammates, get discovered and upvoted by the community
  • Comments & upvotes — threaded discussions on every project
  • Developer dashboard — manage profile, projects and extensions, track coding stats, badges and notifications
  • Discovery — browse and filter developers & projects by language, tools and platform
  • Light & dark mode — full theme support across the platform

Privacy

This plugin is open source. Your source code, file contents, keystrokes, and IP address are never sent. File paths are only sent relative to the git repo root — never an absolute home path. Local privacy flags (hide_file_names, hide_branch_names, hide_project_names) live in ~/.devglobe/config.toml. Your API key is stored in the IDE's native credential manager (PasswordSafe) and mirrored to ~/.devglobe/config.toml so the bundled core process can read it.

Compatible with all JetBrains IDEs: IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, Rider, PhpStorm, CLion, RubyMine, DataGrip, Android Studio, RustRover.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
2.0.0

  • Multi-provider repo URL detection (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, self-hosted Gitea/Forgejo, Azure DevOps).
  • Pure file-system git detection — no git binary required, ~1 ms latency.
  • Sub-second file switching now correctly tracked (no more lost micro-edits).
  • File paths sent only when in a git repository, always relative to the repo root.
  • Privacy flags hide_file_names, hide_branch_names, hide_project_names in ~/.devglobe/config.toml.
  • Visibility settings (anonymous mode, repo sharing, profile mode) moved to the dashboard at devglobe.app/dashboard/settings.
  • Robust core process recovery, automatic retry on download failure.

action.description: Set your status message on DevGlobe
